hard.next_step: 2 Jul: follow-up email to Andrew Waterhouse for a 2nd-week-July meeting; Andy said nothing required from Zycus at this stage. 18 Jun: Carl Kimball re-connected with Andy and drafted a Brien Rodrigues intro to reach Abdulaziz Alsuwailem (SVP / rep-stated Economic Buyer). 13 May: Andy confirmed no update on the RFP. 15 Mar: RFI submitted.
hard.ais_why: NextStep names Merlin Intake as a demo focus, but rep-positioned alongside traditional S2C, not buyer-requested. Buyer conversation centres on KSA hosting and Efficio's vendor-agnostic posture, not AI. No buyer quote names AI as an evaluation criterion. Confidence Medium; score holds at 5 (boundary down).

north_star_verdict.headline: A greenfield six-vendor S2C RFP run by vendor-agnostic Efficio: no PIF champion, the SVP economic buyer never reached, and PIF's own RFP decision stalled since 13 Apr. The 22 Jun Carl Kimball re-connect with Andy Waterhouse reopened the senior channel, but 264 days in Formal Evaluation with no shortlist, demo, or commercials makes the 31 Jul close date not credible.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 13 Oct 2025, 266 days old, entire life in Formal Evaluation (no stage progression). RFI submitted 15 Mar 2026; buyer's RFP decision 'pending' since 13 Apr 2026 (84 days). Last buyer touch 22 Jun 2026 (Andy Waterhouse replied to Carl Kimball's re-connect, 14 days ago) so the deal is engaged, not cold. Close date 31 Jul 2026 is 25 days out; the remaining path (RFP decision, shortlist, demo, EB engagement, commercials, ROI, KSA-hosting sign-off, MSA/SOW) cannot complete in 25 days on a normal 90-day-plus enterprise S2C cycle. Verdict is Slowing (a key action, PIF's RFP decision, is stalled while the buyer stays reachable), not Off Track (no 60-day silence).
evidence: PIF's own RFP decision stalled since 13 Apr 2026: Andy Waterhouse said 'the RFI has been reviewed and a decision about the RFP pending' (SF task/Next_Step__c, 13 Apr 2026); re-confirmed 'No update on the RFP' 13 May 2026.

Senior channel reopened 22 Jun 2026: Andy Waterhouse replied warmly to Carl Kimball's 18 Jun re-connect ('Good to meet you Hakim... would be good to have comms through'), and asked all comms be routed through Carl, Dan and Hakim (SF task, 22 Jun 2026).

The BD intro to the SVP economic buyer misfired: Andy noted 'half the people you have copied below are no longer with the PIF' (SF task, 22 Jun 2026), so the 18 Jun Brien Rodrigues intro to Abdulaziz Alsuwailem did not land.

No PIF internal champion; all access mediated by vendor-agnostic Efficio, which declined a Zycus Alliances influence attempt 20 Mar 2026: 'not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic' (SF Next_Step__c, 20 Mar 2026).
KSA local hosting flagged as a gating requirement 8 Oct 2025 ('will require KSA hosting') with no written Zycus commitment or timeline logged (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025).
No commercials, pricing, ROI, or demo has occurred; the $200k amount is a Salesforce estimate (raised 160k to 200k on 16 Mar 2026 by Dan Quinn), not a buyer-confirmed budget.

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists. The RFI is titled 'Enhance PD Digital Capabilities & Review ERP Solutions - SR-292' (SF task, 15 Mar 2026), which points at replacing or augmenting a legacy Oracle EBS stack, and the rep planned to 'share Oracle EBS vs modern S2C capability-gap matrix' (SF Next_Step__c), but no PIF stakeholder has stated a cost, cycle-time, headcount, or savings target on any call or field. No Avoma buyer call has been transcribed to capture pain in the buyer's own words. Twenty-five days from a placeholder close, there is no financial anchor to justify the $200k or to build SVP sponsorship; extracting the business case (cost of the current Oracle EBS process, target savings, digital-capability KPIs) at the demo is the move.
sources: SF task RFI SR-292 title, 15 Mar 2026

economic_buyer.narrative: The rep identified Abdulaziz Alsuwailem (SVP) as the economic buyer in Next_Step__c on 18 Jun 2026 and drafted a Brien Rodrigues intro to reach him, but he is not on an OpportunityContactRole, has never been engaged, and his identity is unconfirmed outside the rep's free-text note. The 22 Jun re-connect surfaced a real obstacle: Andy Waterhouse said 'half the people you have copied below are no longer with the PIF' (SF task, 22 Jun 2026), so the intro chain to the SVP broke on departed contacts, and Andy redirected all comms through Carl, Dan and Hakim. Budget authority above Andy is therefore mapped only by name, not accessed. The move is to re-route the SVP introduction through Andy's now-confirmed channel and validate his authority on the first call.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026 (SVP / Economic Buyer named)

decision_criteria.narrative: Two structural criteria are visible, both from the rep-buyer trail rather than a documented buyer scorecard. First, KSA local data hosting is a stated gate: on 8 Oct 2025 PIF said the exercise 'will require KSA hosting' and the rep noted Zycus 'might want to invest in local hosting earlier' (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025). Second, the evaluation is a formal RFI/RFP covering the 'whole S2P market' (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025), run by Efficio on a vendor-agnostic basis, so criteria will be scored comparatively across six vendors. What is NOT confirmed is the weighting, the functional must-haves, or where AI/Merlin sits in the scoring; no PIF stakeholder has shared a requirements matrix. Recovering the RFP evaluation criteria from Andy is a ranked move.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 8 Oct 2025 (KSA hosting, whole S2P market)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a PIF-run, Efficio-facilitated formal sourcing exercise for PIF itself (not its portfolio companies): Efficio flagged the 'high-level KSA opportunity' 10 Sep 2025, Zycus met Andre, Errol and two Efficio consultants at PIF Tower 8 Oct 2025, the RFI (SR-292) issued and was submitted 15 Mar 2026, and a decision on whether to proceed to RFP has been 'pending' since 13 Apr 2026. The gatekeeping layer is clear from the call/email sequence: Efficio controls the vendor-agnostic evaluation, Andy Waterhouse (PIF Procurement) is the buyer-side coordinator, and as of 22 Jun 2026 he asked all comms be routed through Carl, Dan and Hakim. What is unknown is the RFP timeline, the approval chain above Andy, and whether a shortlist stage exists before award. The next process milestone Zycus can force is the RFP-decision date and the S2C overview demo.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in play; the deal has not reached commercials. The only paper touched so far is upstream: an NDA / Code of Conduct and vendor registration on PIF's supplier portal (Vendor Gate), which caused repeated access problems in Mar 2026 that Dan Quinn had to work around by emailing the technical proposal directly (SF tasks, 5-15 Mar 2026). As a KSA sovereign-fund, new-business S2C deal, an eventual close will need KSA-entity contracting, an MSA plus Order Form and a separately signed SOW, plus data-residency/local-hosting commitments and likely InfoSec review, none of which has started. Visibility will come only once the RFP decision unlocks commercials; there is no contract-expiration forcing function on record.
sources: SF tasks 5-15 Mar 2026 (Vendor Gate registration, RFI submission)
SF Next_Step__c 12 Jan 2026 (NDA, Code of Conduct)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is qualitative and inferred, not quantified by a named owner. The RFI scope, 'Enhance PD Digital Capabilities & Review ERP Solutions' (SF task, 15 Mar 2026), and the rep's planned 'Oracle EBS vs modern S2C capability-gap matrix' (SF Next_Step__c) together imply PIF's Procurement Department runs a legacy/fragmented Oracle EBS process it wants to modernise as part of a broader digital-capability and KSA-hosting initiative. Efficio framed it as a strategic 'high-level KSA opportunity in 2026' (SF Next_Step__c, 10 Sep 2025), a planned modernisation rather than a crisis. No PIF stakeholder has articulated a specific bottleneck, error rate, or cost of inaction on a call, and the pain owner (procurement transformation lead) is not confirmed. Quantifying the Oracle EBS pain with Andy or the SVP is the move.
sources: SF task RFI SR-292 title, 15 Mar 2026
SF Next_Step__c 10 Sep 2025, capability-gap matrix line

champion.narrative: Zycus has no champion inside PIF. Andy Waterhouse (PIF Shared Services Division, Procurement Department) is responsive and is the warmest relationship, replying to Carl Kimball's re-connect on 22 Jun 2026 and coordinating the RFI, but he is a neutral coordinator executing a vendor-agnostic process, not an advocate: he has repeatedly said there is no RFP update and told Zycus 'nothing required from Zycus team at this stage' (SF Next_Step__c, 2 Jul 2026). The evaluation itself is mediated by Efficio, which explicitly declined to be influenced ('not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic', SF Next_Step__c, 20 Mar 2026). No PIF stakeholder has pushed internally for Zycus, shared scoring intelligence, or introduced the economic buyer. Converting Andy from coordinator to coach, and using the reopened senior channel to build a second thread, is the top structural move.

competition.narrative: This is an open six-vendor RFP: Competitors__c names Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and 'Others', and PIF confirmed the exercise will 'involve whole S2P market' (SF Next_Step__c, 8 Oct 2025). No buyer has stated a preference or ranked any vendor, and Zycus has zero intelligence on scoring because Efficio runs it vendor-agnostically, so specific threat ranking is inference, not buyer evidence. On regional context, SAP Ariba is the likely front-runner given its incumbency and existing KSA hosting footprint in Gulf sovereign/enterprise accounts, with GEP a strong regional peer, but neither is buyer-confirmed here. The wedge is Merlin AI (intake automation, negotiation and supplier intelligence) plus a genuine KSA local-hosting commitment against legacy workflow rivals, but with no champion to carry it and no validated AI appetite, the wedge is theoretical until the demo lands.

deal_movement.summary: The buyer-side RFP decision has been stalled since 13 Apr 2026, but Zycus reopened a senior relationship on 22 Jun 2026 when Carl Kimball (RVP manager) re-connected with Andy Waterhouse, who replied warmly and asked to consolidate comms. The last three months are Zycus-initiated nurture (Summit resources, Horizon invite, Eid greeting) against a buyer holding pattern; the substantive catch is that the intro chain to the SVP economic buyer broke because half the previously copied PIF contacts have left.

items.change: Carl Kimball (RVP manager) re-connected with Andy Waterhouse; Andy replied warmly and asked all comms route through Carl, Dan and Hakim
Andy Waterhouse flagged that half the PIF contacts copied on the SVP intro have left PIF, breaking the Brien Rodrigues intro chain to Abdulaziz Alsuwailem
Close date pushed from 29 Jun to 31 Jul 2026 by Dan Quinn (second slip; RFP decision still pending)
Andy Waterhouse re-confirmed 'No update on the RFP' after the Summit invite exchange
Ownership transferred from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim (RVP); Dan retained then removed as co-owner
RFI (SR-292) response submitted by email after PIF Vendor Gate portal failures
Amount raised from $160k to $200k by Dan Quinn

competitive_position.summary: PIF is running an open, vendor-agnostic six-vendor S2C RFP (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, Others per Competitors__c, 'whole S2P market' per 8 Oct 2025). No PIF stakeholder has expressed a preference and Zycus has no scoring visibility because Efficio blocks influence, so this run adds no new competitive evidence and threat levels are carried forward, not re-ranked. On regional inference SAP Ariba is the strongest likely current threat (Gulf incumbency plus existing KSA hosting), with GEP the peer regional threat; both are unconfirmed by buyer evidence. Zycus wins only by turning Merlin AI plus a concrete KSA local-hosting commitment into a differentiated demo and securing a champion to carry it against a field of six.

competitors.quote: Named in the six-vendor RFP field; PIF said the exercise will 'involve whole S2P market' (8 Oct 2025). No buyer preference stated; regional-incumbency inference only.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin AI intake and negotiation plus a genuine KSA local-hosting commitment against Ariba's legacy workflow and data-residency friction.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Named in Competitors__c; strong Gulf consulting and S2P software presence. No buyer preference evidence.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus single-platform S2P plus Merlin against GEP's services-led motion; anchor on AI depth and local hosting.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Named in Competitors__c. No buyer mention or preference on any call/field.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead on S2C sourcing/contracting depth and KSA hosting where Coupa is P2P-weighted and US-hosted.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: Named in Competitors__c. No buyer mention or preference on any call/field.
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on Merlin AI and faster time-to-value against iValua's configuration-heavy deployments.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Named in Competitors__c. No buyer mention or preference on any call/field.
competitors.how_we_win: Position unified S2P plus Merlin against JAGGAER's modular sourcing focus.

competitors.name: Others
competitors.quote: Unspecified additional vendors in the whole-S2P-market RFP per Competitors__c.
competitors.how_we_win: Surface the full shortlist from Andy once the RFP decision lands.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: PIF's stated expectations centre on KSA local data hosting and modernising a legacy Oracle EBS procurement stack through a vendor-agnostic evaluation. Zycus is aligned on the modernisation goal but exposed on the hosting commitment (unconfirmed) and on the neutral, Efficio-controlled selection where it has no advocate.

best_practice_check.summary: The three levers that move win probability now: build a real PIF champion off the reopened senior channel, re-route the stalled SVP economic-buyer introduction, and convert the KSA-hosting requirement plus Merlin AI into a differentiated demo before the RFP decision.
flags: No internal PIF advocate: all access runs through vendor-agnostic Efficio; use the 22 Jun Carl-Andy re-connect to turn Andy Waterhouse from coordinator into a coach for RFP-scoring visibility.
SVP economic buyer (Abdulaziz Alsuwailem) never reached and the 18 Jun intro misfired on departed PIF contacts; re-route the introduction through Andy's confirmed Carl/Dan/Hakim channel.
Zero competitive intelligence in a six-vendor field; differentiate on Merlin AI intake/negotiation plus a concrete KSA local-hosting commitment at the S2C demo before the RFP decision lands.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Andy Waterhouse is responsive and is the strongest relationship after the 22 Jun re-connect, but he is a neutral RFI coordinator who says nothing is required from Zycus, and the whole evaluation is mediated by vendor-agnostic Efficio, which declined to be influenced on 20 Mar 2026. In a six-vendor RFP with no internal advocate, Zycus is blind to scoring and reactive; converting Andy into a coach is the priority.
champion_strength.source: SF tasks 22 Jun 2026, SF Next_Step__c 2 Jul 2026 & 20 Mar 2026, OpportunityContactRole (no Champion role)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin and Merlin Intake are loaded on the deal and named as a demo focus, but purely rep-positioned; no PIF stakeholder has raised AI as an evaluation criterion. The AI story is under-positioned because there has been no demo and no buyer AI-appetite validation.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5 (Moderate). The only AI signal is the rep positioning Merlin Intake alongside traditional S2C; the buyer conversation centres on KSA hosting and Efficio politics, not AI. No buyer quote names AI, so the AI appetite is unproven, not resistant.

items.detail: All buyer access mediated by vendor-agnostic Efficio, which declined Zycus influence; no internal PIF advocate.
SVP economic buyer named but never engaged; intro chain broke on departed PIF contacts.
266 days in Formal Evaluation with the RFP decision stalled since 13 Apr; the 31 Jul close date is not credible and has already slipped twice.
KSA local data-hosting / residency requirement unconfirmed by a written Zycus commitment; a potential disqualifier for a sovereign fund.
$200k is a Salesforce estimate (raised from $160k 16 Mar), never confirmed by PIF; no commercials or ROI discussed.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is engaged but stalled: the buyer's own RFP decision has been pending 84 days and Andy repeatedly reports no update, while Zycus keeps the relationship warm through nurture and the 22 Jun senior re-connect. Momentum depends entirely on PIF releasing the RFP.
items: Buyer RFP decision pending since 13 Apr 2026; 'No update on the RFP' 13 May 2026.
Senior channel reopened 22 Jun 2026 (Andy Waterhouse replied warmly to Carl Kimball).
Andy said 'nothing required from Zycus team at this stage' (2 Jul 2026): rep-side follow-up, not buyer pull.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: PIF is a greenfield six-vendor S2C RFP run by vendor-agnostic Efficio: no PIF champion, the SVP economic buyer has never been reached, and after 266 days in Formal Evaluation the 31 Jul close is not real; the one bright spot is Carl Kimball's 22 Jun re-connect with Andy Waterhouse as Zycus grows its KSA investment.

win_position.text: Zycus is one of six vendors in a vendor-agnostic RFP with no internal PIF advocate, no scoring visibility, and no validated preference, so it is reacting rather than leading the evaluation.

The SVP economic buyer (Abdulaziz Alsuwailem, named only in the rep's 18 Jun note) has never been engaged and the introduction misfired because half the copied PIF contacts have left (Andy Waterhouse, 22 Jun 2026).
Efficio explicitly refused to be influenced ('not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic', 20 Mar 2026), removing the usual consultant-coach path to shape the shortlist.

The relationship is alive and the senior door reopened: Andy Waterhouse replied warmly to Carl Kimball's 18 Jun re-connect and asked to consolidate comms (22 Jun 2026) as Zycus increases KSA investment.

deal_momentum.text: PIF's own RFP decision has been 'pending' since 13 Apr 2026 and Andy re-confirmed 'No update on the RFP' on 13 May, so no evaluation step has advanced in three months.
No demo, commercials, pricing, or ROI has occurred and Andy said 'nothing required from Zycus team at this stage' (2 Jul 2026), a rep-side follow-up rather than a buyer pull.
The 22 Jun 2026 Carl Kimball re-connect reopened a dormant senior thread and gives Zycus a concrete channel (Carl/Dan/Hakim) to push the RFP timeline.

customer_commitment.text: PIF has committed to nothing beyond running a formal exercise; the $200k is a Zycus estimate (raised from $160k on 16 Mar), not a buyer-confirmed budget.
The buyer has not named a decision date, requested a next meeting, or shared evaluation criteria, so commitment signals are absent.
PIF did invest effort in the process, issuing RFI SR-292 and accepting Zycus's technical submission despite Vendor Gate portal failures (Mar 2026).

deal_risk.text: The 31 Jul 2026 close is not credible: 266 days in Formal Evaluation with no shortlist, demo, or commercials cannot compress into 25 days, and the date has already slipped twice.
The KSA local-hosting requirement (8 Oct 2025) is still unconfirmed by a written commitment and could disqualify Zycus with a sovereign fund if left open.

customer_preference.evidence: Vendor-agnostic RFP, no PIF preference stated; access blocked by Efficio (20 Mar 2026).

champion_strength.evidence: No internal advocate; Andy is a neutral coordinator, Efficio declined influence.

economic_buyer_access.evidence: SVP named but never engaged; 18 Jun intro misfired on departed contacts (22 Jun 2026).

momentum.evidence: RFP decision stalled 84 days; offset slightly by 22 Jun senior re-connect.

scope_change.detail: No narrowing; product scope and amount have held or grown since qualification, consistent with the whole-S2P-market RFP.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative modernisation pain only (legacy Oracle EBS, 'Enhance PD Digital Capabilities' RFI); no cost, savings, or ROI quantified by any PIF stakeholder.
